facts.dependency_basis: is homebound and dependent after a stroke
facts.relationship_to_transferee: a neighbor who became a caregiver
facts.consideration_paid: a nominal sum far below value

docket.entry: Complaint to void the transfer and demand for relief filed.
Answer and counterclaim for the value of care filed.
Order appointing a guardian ad litem for the transferor entered.
Order compelling the transferor's medical records entered.
Court-ordered capacity evaluation completed and report served.
Hearing held on the motion establishing the statutory presumption.

motions.title: Motion for a constructive trust and lis pendens
motions.relief: A constructive trust and a recorded lis pendens preserving the property pending judgment.

motions.title: Motion for appointment of a guardian ad litem
motions.relief: Appointment of a guardian ad litem to protect the transferor's interests.

motions.title: Motion to compel the transferor's medical records
motions.relief: An order compelling production of the transferor's medical and cognitive records.

counterclaims.claim: Quantum meruit for the value of care provided
counterclaims.relief: Compensation for the reasonable value of care and services furnished to the transferor if the deed is set aside.

causes_of_action.claim: Improvident transfer / statutory presumption of undue influence
elements: The transferor was an elderly dependent person
Who made a major transfer of property for less than full consideration
In a confidential or fiduciary relationship and without independent counsel
Triggering a presumption of undue influence the transferee must rebut
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Rescission of the deed and an order setting the transfer aside under 33 M.R.S. § 1023

causes_of_action.against: Maria Q. Fairbanks and Ingrid B. Kingsley
elements: A relationship of trust and confidence
Susceptibility of the transferor to influence
Opportunity and disposition to exert influence
A transfer that reflects the influence rather than the transferor's free will
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order voiding the deed as the product of undue influence

causes_of_action.governing_law: Common law; capacity to execute a deed
elements: The transferor executed the deed
While lacking the mental capacity to understand its nature and effect
Due to the transferor's cognitive condition
Rendering the conveyance voidable
causes_of_action.relief_sought: A declaration that the deed is void for want of capacity

causes_of_action.against: Maria Q. Fairbanks and Ingrid B. Kingsley
elements: The transferee holds title obtained through an improvident transfer
Retention of the property would unjustly enrich the transferee
Equity requires the property be held for the transferor
A constructive trust is the appropriate remedy
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Imposition of a constructive trust and an order returning the property to the transferor

elements: The transferor owned personal property and funds
The transferee exercised dominion over them
Without authorization and inconsistent with the transferor's rights
Causing the transferor loss
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Damages for the value of personal property and funds converted

affirmative_defenses.defense: Full and fair consideration through years of care
affirmative_defenses.basis: Years of care and support furnished by the transferee were full and fair consideration for the transfer.
The transferor ratified and confirmed the conveyance after it was made.
The transferor competently and voluntarily intended the conveyance as a gift.
The transferor understood the nature and effect of the deed and was advised of the transaction. 33 M.R.S. § 1022.
